title: Pouvoirs de négociation et de signature du Royaume-Uni
text_preview: Whereas, for the better treating of and arranging certain matters which are now in discussion between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Governments of certain other Powers and States, relative to the further amendment of the International Convention relating to International Exhibitions, open for signature at Paris from the Iwenty-second day of November, One thousand…

pages.text:
Whereas, for the better treating of and arranging certain matters which are now in discussion between the Government of the United

Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Governments of certain other Powers and

States, relative to the further amendment of the

International Convention relating to International

Exhibitions, open for signature at Paris from the

Iwenty-second day of November, One thousand Nine hundred and Iwenty-eight, to the Thirtieth day of

April, One thousand Nine hundred and Iwenty-nine, it is expedient that a fit person should be invested with Full Power to conduct the said discussion on the part of the Government of the

United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern

Ireland: I, George Alfred Brown, a Member of Her

Majesty's Most Honourable Privy Council, etc., etc., Her Majesty's Principal Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, do hereby certify that

Donald Arthur Logan, Esquire, Companion of the

Most Distinguished Order of Saint Michael and

Saint George, a Counsellor in Her Majesty's

Embassy at Paris, is by these Presents named, constituted and appointed as Plenipotentiary and

Representative having Full Power and Authority to agree and conclude with such Plenipotentiaries and

Representatives as may be vested with similar

Power and Authority on the part of the Governments of certain other Powers and States any Treaty,

Convention

Convention, Agreement,

Protocol or other

Instrument that may tend to the attainment of the above-mentioned end, and to sign for the

Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland everything so agreed upon and concluded. Further, I do hereby certify that whatever things shall be so transacted and concluded by the said Plenipotentiary and

Representative shall be agreed to, acknowledged and accepted by the said Government of the United

Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland in the fullest manner.

In witness whereof I have signed these

Presents and affixed hereto my Seal.

Signed and sealed at the Foreign Office,

London, the Eleventh day of October, One thousand

Nine hundred and Sixty-six.

1 unlo

Whereas, for the better treating of and arranging certain matters which are now in discussion between the Government of the United

Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Governments of certain other Powers and

States, relative to the further amendment of the

International Convention relating to International

Exhibitions, open for signature at Paris from the

Twenty-second day of November, One thousand Nine hundred and Twenty-eight, to the Thirtieth day of

April, One thousand Nine hundred and Twenty-nine, it is expedient that a fit person should be invested with Full Power to conduct the said discussion on the part of the Government of the

United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern

Ireland: I, George Alfred Brown, a Member of Her

Majesty's Most Honourable Privy Council, etc., etc., Her Majesty's Principal Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, do hereby certify that

Sir D'Arcy Patrick Reilly, Knight Commander of the

Most Distinguished Order of Saint Michael and

Saint George, Officer of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire, Her Majesty's Ambassador

Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary at Paris, is by these Presents named, constituted and appointed as

Plenipotentiary and Representative having Full

Power and Authority to agree and conclude with such Plenipotentiaries and Representatives as may be vested with similar Power and Authority on the

part

part of the Governments of certain other Powers and States any Treaty, Convention, Agreement,

Protocol or other Instrument that may tend to the attainment of the above-mentioned end, and to sign for the Government of the United Kingdom of Great

Britain and Northern Ireland everything so agreed upon and concluded. Further I do hereby certify that whatever things shall be so transacted and concluded by the said Plenipotentiary and

Representative shall be agreed to, acknowledged and accepted by the said Government of the United

Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland in the fullest manner.

In witness whereof I have signed these

Presents and affixed hereto my Seal.

Signed and sealed at the Foreign Office,

London, the Eleventh day of October, One thousand

Nine hundred and Sixty-six.
